behaviors.name: DispatchQueuedMessage
behaviors.trigger: Green-ai: async job picks up ConversationMessage WHERE Status=Queued. Layer 0: called inline from ConversationController after message creation.

steps.action: Load ConversationMessage by Id (verify Status=Queued before gateway call)
steps.layer0_source: ConversationRepository.cs:GetConversationMessage:62 — SQL: SELECT TOP(1) * FROM dbo.ConversationMessages WHERE Id = @Id
steps.transformation_note: Layer 0 does NOT load by Id before dispatch — it passes profileId + ConversationReadModel directly to CreateAndSendConversationMessage. Green-ai: load by Id first (INV_CD_01).

steps.action: Load Conversation dispatch context (phone numbers, gateway provider, CustomerId)
steps.layer0_source: ConversationRepository.cs:GetConversation:121 — SQL: SELECT c.*, cpn.PhoneCode SenderPhoneCode, cpn.PhoneNumber SenderPhoneNumber, cpn.GatewayProvider, cpn.CustomerId FROM Conversations INNER JOIN ConversationPhoneNumbers

steps.action: Call SMS gateway: IOutboundMessageSender.SendMessage → returns SmsLogId (long)
On SUCCESS: UPDATE ConversationMessage SET SmsLogId=@smsLogId, Status=Sent (green-ai: ConversationMessageStatus.Sent=2)
steps.layer0_source: ConversationRepository.cs:UpdateConversationMessageStatus:79 — SQL: if exists (select null from ConversationMessages where SmsLogId=@smsLogId) update ConversationMessages set Status=@status where SmsLogId=@smsLogId
steps.transformation_note: Layer 0 updates by SmsLogId (post-send). Green-ai: update by ConversationMessage.Id (INSERT-FIRST — Id is known before dispatch).

steps.action: On FAILURE (MessageNotSentException): UPDATE ConversationMessage SET Status=Failed (green-ai: ConversationMessageStatus.Failed=4)

behaviors.partial_state_risk: NONE if INSERT-FIRST holds — message row exists (Queued) before any gateway call (green-ai INV_CM_01 from SendConversationReplyHandler.cs:88)
behaviors.clone_risk: HIGH — direct port yields send-first-insert-second anti-pattern (Layer 0 ConversationService.cs:67-84)

behaviors.name: UpdateDeliveryStatus
behaviors.trigger: DLR (Delivery Receipt) batch event from SMS gateway — StatusUpdatesEvent.Updates (IEnumerable<StatusUpdate> each has SmsLogId + StatusCode)

steps.action: Receive StatusUpdatesEvent notification with batch of StatusUpdate(SmsLogId, StatusCode)
Batch UPDATE ConversationMessages SET Status=@status WHERE SmsLogId IN @smsLogIds (grouped by status)
steps.layer0_source: ConversationRepository.cs:UpdateConversationMessageStatuses:84 — SQL: update ConversationMessages set Status=@Status where SmsLogId in @SmsLogIds

behaviors.name: GetConversationMessages
behaviors.sql: SELECT m.Id, m.[Text], m.DateCreatedUtc, m.SmsLogId FROM dbo.ConversationMessages m INNER JOIN dbo.Conversations c ON c.Id = m.ConversationId WHERE c.Id = @conversationId ORDER BY m.Id ASC
behaviors.note: Read behavior — present in Layer 0. No Status filter. Not a dispatch behavior but needed by dispatch context.

behaviors.name: GetQueuedConversationMessages
behaviors.source: NONE — no Layer 0 equivalent. Layer 0 never polls for Queued messages (concept does not exist in Layer 0).
behaviors.design_basis: Green-ai design decision: SELECT cm.* FROM ConversationMessages cm INNER JOIN Conversations c ON c.Id=cm.ConversationId WHERE cm.Status=@Queued AND c.CustomerId=@CustomerId ORDER BY cm.Id ASC — customer isolation must be explicit (DD_CD_03)
behaviors.note: The query pattern is derivable from existing Layer 0 patterns (ConversationRepository GetConversationMessages JOIN + CustomerId from GetConversation) — but the Status=Queued polling behavior does not exist in Layer 0.
